derivations.baseline_tension: lower bound of published resting cell-attached patch range 0.5-4.0 mN/m
derivations.calcium_current_fraction: GHK estimate approximately 0.04 under physiological ion concentrations; calculation recorded in evidence report
derivations.calcium_detection_limit: 10 nM low-end Fura-2 calibration and approximately 2x HUVEC basal SEM
derivations.channel_count: 23.8 pF / (1 uF/cm^2) = 2380 um^2; 2380 um^2 * 1.75 channels/um^2 = 4165 channels
derivations.curvature_radius: mean patch-dome radius 0.8 um used in native-density analysis

notes: Literature-constrained reference parameterization. Several quantities remain cross-cell or model proxies and must be treated as uncertainty variables.

parameter_notes.apical_channel_fraction: 0.5 is neutral because apical localization is qualitative and no numerical endothelial apical/junctional fraction was found.
parameter_notes.calcium_fraction: 0.04 is a pre-run GHK proxy, not a direct measurement.
parameter_notes.channel_count: 4165 is derived before the run from 23.8 pF HUVEC capacitance, 1 uF/cm^2 specific capacitance, and 1.75 channels/um^2 native Neuro2A density; it is not a direct endothelial count.
parameter_notes.fast_fraction: 0.5 is a neutral equal-weight representation because available HUVEC data identify temporal regimes but not generalized-SLS branch amplitudes.
parameter_notes.localization_area: 10 um^2 is the largest observed coarse endothelial cortical mesh area and therefore the conservative end of the local-domain evidence range; smaller areas were not selected after observing results.
parameter_notes.status: All required source groups are populated, while proxy quantities remain explicitly identified.
parameter_notes.transfer_fractions: All three transfer fractions are fixed at 1.0 as favorable physical upper bounds. They are not inferred from the outcome.

sources.calcium_conversion: Carter et al. doi:10.1111/j.1469-7793.1998.845ba.x supports HUVEC Vm=-40+/-3 mV. Yadegari F et al. J Phys Chem B. 2024;128:1139-1160. doi:10.1021/acs.jpcb.3c04534 reports HUVEC isotonic volume 2159.7+/-10.8 um^3. Shen J et al. AJP Cell Physiol. 1992;262:C384-C390. doi:10.1152/ajpcell.1992.262.2.C384 reports shear Ca2+ peaks at 15-40 s and returns toward baseline in 40-80 s, motivating a 20 s first-order clearance proxy. The Ca-current fraction 0.04 is a documented Goldman-Hodgkin-Katz proxy using physiological Na/K/Ca concentrations and reported PIEZO1 Ca permeability; it is not a direct endothelial measurement.
sources.calcium_detection_limit: Wickham NWR et al. Measurement of intracellular calcium concentration in intact monolayers of human endothelial cells. Basal HUVEC [Ca2+]i 117+/-5 nM. Henke W et al. Cell Calcium. 1996;20:287-292. doi:10.1016/S0143-4160(96)90034-2 calibrated Fura-2 down to 10 nM. A 10 nM change is frozen as a conservative imaging-scale detection threshold.
sources.channel_density: Lewis AH, Grandl J. Piezo1 ion channels inherently function as independent mechanotransducers. eLife. 2021;10:e70988. doi:10.7554/eLife.70988. Native Neuro2A density about 1.75 channels/um^2. Carter TD et al. Membrane capacitance changes induced by thrombin and calcium in single HUVEC. J Physiol. 1998;513:845-855. doi:10.1111/j.1469-7793.1998.845ba.x. Mean HUVEC capacitance 23.8 pF. Using the standard 1 uF/cm^2 specific capacitance gives about 2380 um^2 membrane area and 4165 channels. This is a cross-cell literature proxy, not a direct HUVEC PIEZO1 count.
sources.current_detection_limit: Harrison RR et al. Microchip amplifier for in vitro, in vivo, and automated whole-cell patch-clamp recording. J Neurophysiol. 2015;113:1275-1282. doi:10.1152/jn.00629.2014. Current noise 1.1 pA RMS over 5 kHz; a conservative 3-sigma threshold of 3.3 pA is frozen before analysis.
sources.exposure_localization: Pesen D, Hoh JH. doi:10.1529/biophysj.104.049965. The same conservative 10 um^2 upper end of the measured endothelial cortical-mesh area is used for the nonnegative magnitude-exposure pathway. Exposure remains a magnitude/energy input and is never treated as signed force.
sources.normal_mechanics: Vargas-Pinto R et al. The effect of the endothelial cell cortex on atomic force microscopy measurements. Biophys J. 2013;105:300-309. PMCID:PMC3714930; PMID:23870251. HUVEC modulus 3.23+/-0.54 kPa with sharp tips, 0.71+/-0.16 kPa with spherical tips, cortex thickness 312+/-65 nm. Feneberg et al. doi:10.1529/biophysj.103.037044 supplies the independently measured 0.05 and 0.15 s HUVEC relaxation-regime boundaries. The equal fast/slow branch weight is a neutral model-identification convention.
sources.signed_force_localization: Pesen D, Hoh JH. Micromechanical architecture of the endothelial cell cortex. Biophys J. 2005;88:670-679. doi:10.1529/biophysj.104.049965. Endothelial apical cortex coarse mechanical mesh elements span approximately 0.5-10 um^2. The conservative largest reported coherent-domain area, 10 um^2, is used; unit force transfer is an explicit favorable upper bound, not a measured efficiency.
sources.single_channel_conductance: Vascular mechanotransduction. Physiol Rev. 2023;103. doi:10.1152/physrev.00053.2021, summarizing primary PIEZO1 measurements: approximately 30 pS under physiological Ca2+-containing conditions. The endpoint uses 30 pS.
sources.spatial_channel_distribution: Mylvaganam S et al. The spectrin cytoskeleton integrates endothelial mechanoresponses. Nat Cell Biol. 2022;24:1226-1238. doi:10.1038/s41556-022-00953-5. The study supports apical spectrin/caveola-associated PIEZO1 mechanosensing but does not quantify an apical fraction; therefore a neutral 0.5 apical/junctional split is frozen and flagged as an unmeasured spatial proxy.
sources.tangential_mechanics: Feneberg W, Aepfelbacher M, Sackmann E. Microviscoelasticity of the apical cell surface of HUVEC within confluent monolayers. Biophys J. 2004;87:1338-1350. doi:10.1529/biophysj.103.037044. HUVEC fast response t<0.05 s, plateau 0.05-0.15 s, surface shear modulus 2.5e-4 N/m and inferred bulk modulus about 400 Pa. Mathur AB et al. Atomic force and TIRF microscopy for force transmission in endothelial cells. Biophys J. 2000;78:1725-1735. PMID:10733955. HUVEC edge modulus 1.27+/-0.36 kPa. The equal fast/slow branch weight is a neutral model-identification convention because the papers do not report a two-branch amplitude decomposition.
